title: Carpenters and joiners
description: Construct, erect, install, or repair structures and fixtures made of wood and comparable materials, such as concrete forms; building frameworks, including partitions, joists, studding, and rafters; and wood stairways, window and door frames, and hardwood floors. May also install cabinets, siding, drywall, and batt or roll insulation. Includes brattice builders who build doors or brattices (ventilation walls or partitions) in underground passageways. Repair and open locks, make keys, change locks and safe combinations, and install and repair safes. Set up and operate machines, such as lathes, milling and engraving machines, and jig borers to make working models of metal or plastic objects. Includes template makers. (Based on 11 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Select and order lumber or other required materials.
Maintain job records, document actions, and present written progress reports while scheduling work crew.
Follow established safety rules and maintain a safe and clean environment.
Study specifications in blueprints or sketches to prepare project layout and determine materials required.
Measure, mark cutting lines, and verify dimensions of materials prior to cutting using appropriate tools.
Shape or cut materials to specified measurements using hand tools, machines, or power saws, and install structures or fixtures.
Erect scaffolding or ladders for assembling structures and ensure stability by verifying levels and trueness.
Assemble and fasten materials to create frameworks or props, and install rough door and window frames and fixtures.
Inspect and repair damaged structures, removing defective parts and replacing or fixing them using hand tools.
Dig or direct digging of post holes and set poles to support structures.
Build, repair, or construct wooden fixtures such as cabinets, doors, and frameworks using appropriate tools.
Construct forms or chutes for pouring concrete and perform related foundational work.
Trim, sand, or scrape surfaces to prepare articles for finishing, and finish surfaces of woodwork using paint or sealers.
Select finishing ingredients like paint or varnish suitable for different wood types and apply them appropriately.
Verify and adjust the conformance of finished products to dimensions and specifications using measuring instruments.
Cut new or duplicate keys and install door hardware like locks and closers.
Establish specifications for projects based on designs and discuss project details with customers as needed.
Issue patterns or templates to designated machine operators for production processes.
Maintain records of patterns, equipment, and supplies required for projects and order parts as necessary.
Fit, fasten, and assemble wood parts to form completed units, using glue, screws, nails, or bolts.
Repair and rework templates and patterns, and design and create templates, patterns, or coreboxes according to work orders or mockups.
Assemble pattern sections using hand tools, bolts, screws, rivets, glue, or welding equipment, and align, fit, and join parts with bolts, screws, welding, or gluing.
Lay out and mark reference points and dimensions on materials using measuring instruments and drawing tools, and compute layout dimensions for further processing.
Lift, position, and secure machined parts on surface plates or worktables, and lift and position workpieces relating to surface plates manually or with hoists.
Read and interpret blueprints or drawings, compute dimensions, and plan operational sequences for machining or other processes.
Drill, countersink, and ream holes in parts for fasteners using power tools, and grind, file, and sand parts to finished dimensions.
Devise and construct tools, dies, molds, jigs, and fixtures, or modify existing tools and equipment to meet job requirements.
Set up and operate machine tools, such as milling machines and lathes, for machining castings or patterns.
Inspect machined parts to verify conformance to specifications and record specifications and final dimensions for operating standards.
Identify required electrical and electronic connections and components for assembling mechanical, electrical, and electronic prototypes or models.
Perform preventative maintenance or minor repairs on machines used in fabrication, and modify existing programs for improved efficiency.
Order tooling for jobs and sort shop orders to maximize materials utilization and minimize machine setup time.
